page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting hardware-assisted virtualization and advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server includes 1843 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark evaluations, the D48ds_v5 demonstrates strong performance in several areas, including a Passmark CPU score of 48717.7 and a Passmark database operations score of 21703.24. Memory performance is also strong, with a Passmark memory score of 2757.12. For cryptographic tasks, the hardware-accelerated encryption test scored 43321.1.

While the server excels in image processing and small-scale LLM inference, processing 2335.66 tokens per second for a 135M model, it exhibits performance tradeoffs on larger workloads. Specifically, text generation using a large 70B parameter model is weak, yielding only 2.74 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and high-capacity local storage, the D48ds_v5 is suited for database operations, enterprise application hosting, web serving, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it supports AVX-512 and cryptographic extensions. Benchmark data indicates strong capabilities in database operations, memory performance, and image processing, alongside efficient small-model LLM inference. However, without GPU acceleration, text generation on large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, scoring a weak 2.74 tokens per second. This server offers a balanced resource profile, making it highly suitable for relational databases, enterprise applications, and web serving workloads requiring substantial local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a DDSv5-family virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory benchmark performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, optimized for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs, high-speed local storage, and strong database and memory performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB RAM
Includes 1843 GB of local NVMe storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong benchmark performance in database operations and memory throughput
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Suited for relational databases, web serving, and enterprise applications
Performance limitations on large-scale LLM inference due to lack of GPU
